pi_items.source: Task description line 1 and confirmed in setup_task.sh line 12 and export_result.sh line 27
Task description line 3 and confirmed in export_result.sh line 28 as INDEX_FILE = os.path.join(TARGET_DIR, 'index.txt')

Cross-referenced with task description URLs: https://www.usda.gov/topics/farming (domain: usda.gov), https://www.epa.gov/agriculture (domain: epa.gov), https://www.nass.usda.gov/Quick_Stats/ (domain: nass.usda.gov). All domains verified as real government websites.

Downloaded and verified actual page sizes: EPA agriculture page = 66,605 bytes (65.04 KB), NASS Quick Stats page = 54,488 bytes (53.21 KB). Both pages exceed 5000 byte threshold by large margin. Used in verifier.py line 51 and 67.

This value is declared in task.json metadata but NOT actually used in verifier.py. The verifier instead checks if index content > 150 chars (line 121). This represents an inconsistency between metadata claim and actual verification logic.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires saving three real web pages from government websites (USDA, EPA, NASS) to an offline directory and creating an index file. The required domains are usda.gov, epa.gov, and nass.usda.gov, corresponding to the official USDA, EPA, and NASS websites. The actual web pages at these domains exceed 5000 bytes (EPA page: 66.6 KB, NASS page: 53.2 KB), confirming that the min_html_size_bytes threshold is reasonable. The target directory is /home/ga/Documents/offline_references and the index file is named index.txt.
